reviews.author: Michelle
reviews.text: Great food and cocktails! Fun and energetic atmosphere. Friendly, quick service. I really enjoyed the whipped feta dip appetizer and the bowl options on the menu. The avocado bowl with chicken was delicious and full of flavor.

reviews.author: Tiffany David
reviews.text: Celebrated my 24th birthday here and it was a great time! Apps were amazing, drinks were strong, and the service was excellent. They gave not just me, but the whole table complimentary birthday shots!! We had an issue with one of the food items and they comped it no questions asked. Our server was super kind and he generously took some pictures for us! Perfect place to go for larger gatherings. The vibe was everything and if it's nice out they keep the garage doors open!

reviews.author: S. Krug
reviews.text: First visit.  Average food at bistro prices.  The grilled cheese birria enjoyable, the sides fries chunky not thin.  The Brussels sprouts were a bit oversweet as they included both honey and bits of pie crust.  I would have enjoyed this dish much more without the latter and some balsamic vinegar.  Generous amount.  The server very friendly and divided our sandwich when we said we would split everything without being asked.  When we got our bill, we noticed a 3% surcharge supposedly to smooth out prices.  What?  He offered to remove which we accepted.  Beware of this.  Upbeat setting.

reviews.author: BryDeV
reviews.text: Really good smash burger, a bit pricey for a burger, but very very tasty.  Great beer options, friendly bartenders and staff. Cool place and would eat there again

reviews.author: Kate Quinn
reviews.text:
Merriment Social: The Unforgettable Highlight of Our Wedding Weekend

Choosing Merriment Social for our rehearsal dinner was one of the best planning decisions we made. It set an incredible, celebratory, and fun tone for our entire wedding weekend and truly ended up being one of the major highlights for all of our guests.

Our initial planning partnership with Joe was absolutely wonderful. He was a phenomenal guide in planning the event and, most importantly, in choosing the perfect menu. Joe was incredibly responsive, knowledgeable, and genuinely passionate about creating an experience that reflected our taste. He made the process easy and exciting, and his recommendations were spot on—our guests raved about the food all weekend long!

The vibe and atmosphere at Merriment Social are unmatched. The space is unique, stylish, and immediately puts everyone in a festive mood. Beyond the gorgeous setting, the service provided by the staff was impeccable. They were professional, warm, and attentive to every detail, ensuring drinks were full and plates were cleared seamlessly. They executed the evening flawlessly, allowing us to simply relax and enjoy time with our closest friends and family.

If you are looking for a venue that provides amazing food, unique atmosphere, and a flawless planning and execution experience, Merriment Social is the only choice.

— Jason and Kate

reviews.author: Korinne Mack
reviews.text:
Overrated. I had been in before for a drink but decided to come in for happy hour and maybe an appetizer on the dinner menu. I got the HH specialty cocktail which was good. The shrimp cocktail was nothing to write home about (It was an "elevated version" of a regular shrimp cocktail. I could've taken a regular version honestly). I decided to get the potholes on the dinner menu too. Very rich but incredibly salty. Nearly inedible so I couldn't eat it.

I think the thing that bothered me the most was that the bartender checked on all of the couples at the bar multiple times except for me (prior to the dinner rush so it's not like it was super busy). Basically ignored me for 45 min. Didn't say a word to me until he saw my coat on and I was standing up. Yes, I could have said something if I really wanted to but at a certain point, I'm jw how long it will take to acknowledge me. All the other couples got prompt service. I get I'm a solo diner, but I'd still like good service. Definitely won't be back. There are other seafood spots.

reviews.author: Maxi Harmon
reviews.text: A fantastic find during our stay in Milwaukee—just a short walk from our hotel. We stopped in for small bites and were blown away. The roasted carrot hummus was a standout—unexpectedly delicious and full of flavor. The Brussels sprouts were another surprise—unique and expertly crafted by the chef. The oysters were fresh and incredible. Loved the elegant atmosphere—sophisticated but still relaxed and welcoming.

reviews.author: Samantha Baldyga
reviews.text: Alex was very attentive and he was knowledgeable about the menu items. The atmosphere was nice with the front window open and sitting and watching as people walked by. The steak tartar was delicious with the potato chip topping but a little too salty. The scallops were delicious along with the duck tacos. They were spicy and had a fantastic flavor. We ended with the key lime pie which was also good and just the right amount of dessert. The old fashion was surprisingly good with the aged rum and the other bourbon drink I had was also very delicious.

reviews.author: stuartwogs
reviews.text: Variety of seafood dishes and appetizers. Tried a scallops dish with risotto. Scallops were a little over cooked and could use a push on seasoning. Will try again since the atmosphere and service were great.

reviews.author: A small elf
reviews.text: Wow we went out with the family and some friends for dinner we get there and with four kids and four adults we needed food fast and we got it fast. The waiter helped us get cheese burgers for the younger ones and the food on the menu was delicious I got the aura king salmon and the toast was all there

reviews.author: Jim Whitaker
reviews.text: I will start with the Pros. The atmosphere was awesome! Great vibe and awesome music selection. The building was super clean including the bathrooms. Waitress and hostess were very kind. The menu had some really cool items and loved the specialty pizza ideas. The Cons! The pizza was below mid grade. The crust is extremely dry and stale tasting. Sauce lacked flavor and overall tasted like an old Pizza Hut pizza. I did enjoy the crab spring rolls  and enjoyed the spice. Overall for the price and lack of good pizza I would not recommend.

reviews.author: Alandra Nyree
reviews.text: I tried the Holiday Punch Slushie, it was delicious! We also ordered the Spinach and Artichoke dip, which could’ve been a little creamier, but the Crab Rangoon Spring Rolls were absolutely amazing. To top it all off, the King Cheese Pizza was packed with flavor and the crust and sauce were both perfect. I highly recommend this place to all my friends and family!

reviews.author: Alicia Richter
reviews.text: My boyfriend and I dined here for the first time today, and it was amazing! Leo, who was our hostess/server, provided incredible and attentive service. A gentleman even offered to take our picture when our food arrived, which we appreciated. I highly recommend the Pink Pony drink—it's worth the price! Such an amazing place and we will be back.

reviews.author: Lisa Ridenour
reviews.text: This might be my new favorite pizza place! The food and drinks were creative and tasty and the vibes were immaculate. Do NOT sleep on the cheese stuffed garlic sticks with vodka sauce or the truffle and/or spicy ranch dipping sauce.

reviews.author: Gabriel Huff
reviews.text: Came here for valentines on our day trip to Milwaukee from chi-town. We got a King Cheese and a Curd Your Enthusiasm and some drinks, it was all extremely yummy!!! The service was also amazing, all the staff is very kind, knowledgeable, and professional! Can’t recommend them enough!!

reviews.author: Nick Wolf
reviews.text: We were here for their Valentine's Day special. Their ambiance and service is really nice. The welcome cocktail was pretty good, maybe a little sweet but good. The charcuterie plate was a bit "pedestrian" and reminded us of a Lunchable with Ritz crackers, cubed cheese, and lunch meat. I'm wondering if they went over budget on this prepaid special and that's where they cut costs. The main pasta entree with sausage was delicious. However, for some reason we could not each get a different entree (there were two options), we had to get the same entree, which was not disclosed at purchase. The chocolate fondue dessert was also good. The sharing plates they used didn't fit the atmosphere and reminded me of a cafeteria or diner, but that's a minor detail if the food is good. Improve the charcuterie offering and let each person choose their entree and this would be a 5/5.

reviews.author: Henry Schellhorn
reviews.text: Considered a "speakeasy" but really just a cocktail bar underneath the restaurant. The cocktail menu is rotating. We ended up being there on a night where they had a special bottle of potato-infused gin that they were making cocktails with. All the drinks we tried were really good. Ethan is quite the bartender-he will ask what type of cocktail you like and make one custom for you. Check this place out for some unique, fun, and delicious cocktails.

reviews.author: Lesli Jennings
reviews.text: Drinks were absolutely amazing! I highly recommend this place - very creative, distinct cocktails. Stop in and enjoy the ambiance and the drinks. Mitchell is the best!

reviews.author: Sam Miller
reviews.text: One of the best dirty martinis we’ve had—fantastic olive juice. The bartender, Majir, was incredibly kind and talented. Overall, great experience before our dinner. Can’t wait to come back

reviews.author: Jordan Llanas
reviews.text: I sat at the bar and got to watch my drinks be made! The bartenders were so welcoming and provided quick service. The Loon Room’s menu offered an array of different elevated drink profiles so everyone can find something to enjoy - everything was delicious! Their intimate setting offered modern cozy vibes that everyone in my group enjoyed.
